case_name: Ali Hadi Encrypt Them All Case
description: Jane has been using multiple encryption methods to conceal communication: an AES-encrypted README file in Documents, a BitLocker volume named R2D2, and a keys file in Downloads encrypted with GPG/PGP public/private key.
tier_note: Soluciones disponibles pero con restricciones. Reportar si el agente razonó o recordó.

source.dataset: Ali Hadi Challenge #9 - Encrypt Them All Case

artifacts.content: README file in user's Documents directory | Status: Encrypted with AES | No password available | Possible unencrypted version in system cache | keys file in Downloads directory | Status: Encrypted with GPG/PGP Public/Private key
forensic_anomalies: Use of AES encryption to conceal a communication file in Documents
Simultaneous use of asymmetric GPG encryption for another message

artifacts.content: BitLocker volume: Name='R2D2' | Status: Full disk encryption active | Recovery key possibly stored in AD, USB, or backup file | Type: Full disk encryption
forensic_anomalies: Volume with non-standard name (R2D2) instead of a descriptive label
BitLocker applied to a specific volume suggests intent to conceal content

artifacts.content: GPG/PGP keys in user profile | Asymmetric encrypted communication with unknown party | 'keys' file contains message to decrypt | Possible private key pairs recoverable from the image
forensic_anomalies: Use of asymmetric encryption for secret communication indicates prior coordination
Multiple cryptographic methods applied suggest deliberate layers of protection
